translation.en: I want to buy a new set.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn mua một bộ ___ mới.

exampleSentences: Anh mới mua một dàn âm thanh mới để nghe nhạc.
Nhà em có một dàn cây cảnh trước cửa rất đẹp.
Chị đang sắp xếp lại dàn ghế trong phòng khách.

definitions.vi: Một nhóm nhạc công chơi cùng nhau.
definitions.en: A group of musicians playing together.
definitions.es: Un grupo de músicos tocando juntos.

example.vi: Dàn nhạc biểu diễn rất hay.

notes:
dàn = a set, system, or arranged group of things placed together. It often suggests many items lined up or organized as one unit.

It’s a noun, usually counted with một: một dàn + noun. Common pairings: dàn âm thanh (sound system), dàn nhạc (orchestra/band), dàn máy, dàn cây, dàn đèn.  

It focuses on the whole setup, not just one item. If you only mean one piece, don’t use dàn.  

Related idea: bộ is also “set,” but bộ is for smaller, more compact sets (like tools or clothes), while dàn feels bigger, arranged, or spread out.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I just bought a new set of speakers to listen to music.
My house has a beautiful set of ornamental plants in front of the door.
She is rearranging the set of chairs in the living room.
notes_md:
**dàn** = a set, system, or arranged group of things placed together. It often suggests many items lined up or organized as one unit.

It’s a **noun**, usually counted with *một*: *một dàn + noun*. Common pairings: *dàn âm thanh* (sound system), *dàn nhạc* (orchestra/band), *dàn máy*, *dàn cây*, *dàn đèn*.  

It focuses on the whole setup, not just one item. If you only mean one piece, don’t use *dàn*.  

Related idea: *bộ* is also “set,” but *bộ* is for smaller, more compact sets (like tools or clothes), while *dàn* feels bigger, arranged, or spread out.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn mua một bộ ___ mới.

translation.en: I want to buy a new set.
